input.counterSource: No credible counter-source found. SAP Fiori uses "Cozy/Compact" terminology (https://www.sap.com/design-system/fiori-design-web/v1-96/foundations/visual/cozy-compact) and Material Design uses its own density scale, but neither contradicts the Cloudscape-specific claim — they are simply different systems with different naming.
input.evidence: The Cloudscape documentation at https://cloudscape.design/foundation/visual-foundation/content-density/ confirms the definition verbatim: "Content density is defined by the ratio of information visible compared to the space available in the interface." It explicitly names exactly two modes: Comfortable ("the standard density level of the system, active by default") and Compact ("an additional density level for data intensive views"). The claim is a faithful paraphrase of the primary source. No contradicting evidence was found — other design systems (SAP Fiori, Material Design) use different naming conventions but do not dispute Cloudscape's own documented behavior. The source is Cloudscape's own production documentation (an AWS design system), which is an appropriate primary source for a claim about Cloudscape's features. The content is actively maintained and corroborated by 2024–2025 design system articles. No marketing inflation, no overreach from the quote.
